title: Who controls the Berserk Unit 01
wikitext:
[]: Who controls the 'Berserk' Unit 01?             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Wed Jul 19, 2006 4:51 pm I put berzerk in quotes because when we seen her go 'out of control', its always for a focused purpose. Why is ask this is because I'm trying to get to the heart of the relationship between Yui and the Creature himself. Are the evangelions animals, who if allowed, would instinctive act, to eat, to gratify themselves? Some hypothesize that this is how we would be without a soul. Is the soul needed to keep these feral insticts at bay? Or is the soul needed to needed to get the Creature to move, like a key for the car? And the role of the pilot, is to control the Creature using the Entry Plug. Passing on desires to move. What i mean is that as im typing this to you, i want to express my thoughts, so my fingers move accordingly. And with the case of the Dummy Plug. Does it bypass the pilot, or both the pilot and the resident soul? I personally dont think that a sweet woman like Yui would be able to act in the way the 'berzerk' Unit 01 does. does this hold water?

[]:             Quote Post by Kaysow » Wed Jul 19, 2006 5:09 pm BÃ¤rsÃ¤rk - If you wanna spell it all fancy

[]:             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Wed Jul 19, 2006 5:13 pm lollerskates

[]: Re: Who controls the 'Berzerk' Unit 01?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 19, 2006 5:28 pm InvaderRen wrote: Why is ask this is because I'm trying to get to the heart of the relationship between Yui and the Creature himself.

[]:             Quote Post by tinmeigut » Wed Jul 19, 2006 9:59 pm but I remember in the manga version it was said that Shinji himself piloted the Eva, but not the soul of Yui. However I think Reichu's explanation is more believable, as a first-time pilot probably won't know how to conrtol the Eva so well.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Wed Jul 19, 2006 10:18 pm I personally dont think that a sweet woman like Yui would be able to act in the way the 'berzerk' Unit 01 does.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 20, 2006 12:32 am Defectron wrote: If you spent most of your time stuck inside a big mecha looking thing unable to move 99% of the time, you'd be pretty pissed off too.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Thu Jul 20, 2006 1:40 am Do you have a mother? Does she care for you? Well I've seen my mom go berserk a couple of times when one of us (her children ) was harmed by someone, or we were on to spill guano on ourselves. And those times when Unit-01 goes berserk, Shinji is knee-deep in trouble. And what else can a mother do? Defend the child, whatever the cost. This is a mother's job after all. And a father's too, but let not this topic run this social.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 20, 2006 2:01 am Granted, Yui's actions are a bit more complicated than "protect child", in the grand scheme. Eating Zeruel was part of a bigger plan. And perhaps, too, when she did what any loving mother would do and took her son straight into Hell. "Hop in, Shinji. Or else." "Where are we going?" "To Disneyland." "Really?" "Yeah, sure. Now shut up and get into the thing sticking painfully out of my back already."

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Thu Jul 20, 2006 6:30 am Reichu wrote: Eating Zeruel was part of a bigger plan.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 20, 2006 1:37 pm FauÃŸtin wrote: She went into Unit-01 in order to protect her son as well as she could.

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Thu Jul 20, 2006 1:41 pm Defectron wrote: If you spent most of your time stuck inside a big mecha looking thing unable to move 99% of the time, you'd be pretty pissed off too.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 20, 2006 1:44 pm ZeroFive1 wrote: And if you had to injure your son's friend against your will, yeah.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Thu Jul 20, 2006 1:45 pm ZeroFive1 wrote: Defectron wrote: If you spent most of your time stuck inside a big mecha looking thing unable to move 99% of the time, you'd be pretty pissed off too.

[]:             Quote Post by darth_mark » Fri Jul 21, 2006 9:52 am it would've been funny if it was revealed that yui actually went "berserk" all the time even when she was still human. "rough night gendo?" - fuyutsuki "yeah.. yui went "berserk" again.." - gendo lucky bastard - fuyutsuki

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 21, 2006 1:13 pm FauÃŸtin wrote: And seeing your family falling apart, and your husband becoming an asshole.

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Fri Jul 21, 2006 1:23 pm lol I could imagine a Rabid-Drunken-Yui breaking Gendo's arm the same way she broke Sachiel's- all her experience with beserk fighting came from when she was human! Which explains how she could fight with such "grace" in Unit 01

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Jul 22, 2006 8:40 am Perhaps we should all take a moment and ask ourselves; is the question, "Who is in control when X goes berserk?", actually well posed?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 22, 2006 11:37 am defiate wrote: I could imagine a Rabid-Drunken-Yui breaking Gendo's arm the same way she broke Sachiel's

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sat Jul 22, 2006 2:52 pm Reichu wrote: defiate wrote: I could imagine a Rabid-Drunken-Yui breaking Gendo's arm the same way she broke Sachiel's
summary: []: Who controls the 'Berserk' Unit 01?             Quote Post by InvaderRen » Wed Jul 19, 2006 4:51 pm I put berzerk in quotes because when we seen her go 'out of control', its always for a focused purpose. Why is ask this is because I'm trying to get to the heart of the relationship between Yui and the Creature himself. Are the evangelions animals, who if allowed, would instinctive act, to eat, to gratify themselves? Some hypo
